item.title: US Easter spending to touch a record $24.9 billion in 2026, NRF forecasts
item.description: U.S. consumer spending on Easter is expected to rise to a record $24.9 ​billion in 2026, a 5.5% increase ‌from a year earlier, industry body National Retail Federation said on Tuesday, signaling robust demand ​amid economic uncertainty.

item.title: Norway wealth fund moves towards some AI-driven decisions but with humans in control
item.description: Norway's $2.1 trillion sovereign wealth fund, the world's ​largest, will eventually allow some investment decisions to be made by AI systems under human supervision, ‌but not yet, as the tools still make errors, fund officials said on Tuesday.

item.title: BMO to launch tokenized cash platform with CME and Google Cloud
item.description: Bank of Montreal said on Tuesday it plans to roll out tokenized cash capabilities in collaboration with CME Group and ​Google Cloud, as it seeks to meet rising demand ‌for real‑time payments amid a global shift toward around‑the‑clock trading.

item.title: Barclays raises 2026 year-end S&P 500 target to 7,650 despite Middle East, inflation risks
item.description: Barclays raised its 2026 year-end S&P 500 target on Tuesday, betting that strong corporate earnings led by ​the technology sector and resilient economic growth will outweigh rising ‌macro risks, including war in the Middle East, AI-driven disruption and stress emerging in private credit markets.

item.title: United Airlines bets bigger on premium travel as Iran war drives up fuel costs
item.description: United Airlines on Tuesday ​unveiled a broad aircraft and cabin overhaul centered on higher-end seating, pressing ahead with ‌its long-term growth plan days after warning that oil could stay above $100 a barrel through 2027 and outlining plans to trim some flights in response.

item.title: Aspex wants Delivery Hero CEO out as investor's campaign heats up
item.description: Aspex, a top Delivery Hero shareholder, on Tuesday urged the online takeaway ​food group's supervisory board to oust ‌the CEO and his top management team in an escalation of the investor's campaign for a ​change in strategic direction.

item.title: Morning Bid: From 48 hours to five days
item.description: What matters in U.S. and global markets today

item.title: US stock futures dip as Middle East uncertainty tempers relief rally
item.description: U.S. stock index futures were down on ​Tuesday, after a relief rally in the previous session, as renewed doubts over easing Middle East ‌tensions weighed on sentiment despite President Donald Trump's decision to delay strikes on Iran's power grid.

item.title: Smithfield Foods beat quarterly results estimates on resilient demand for pork
item.description: Smithfield Foods beat analysts' estimates for fourth-quarter sales and profit on Tuesday, helped by resilient demand for packaged meats ​and cost-saving efforts, lifting the U.S. pork processor's shares ‌nearly 4% higher in premarket trading.

item.title: Euro zone growth nearly stalls as Middle East war fuels inflation surge, PMI shows
item.description: Euro zone private sector growth slowed sharply in March as the Middle East war drove input costs to their highest in more than three years and triggered the worst supply chain ​disruptions since mid-2022, a survey showed on Tuesday.

item.title: China banks eye profit boost as nearly $8 trillion in deposits to be repriced
item.description: China's leading state-owned banks are expected to recover this year from record-low profit margins as the repricing of nearly $8 trillion worth of ​maturing high-priced time deposits eases pressure on their funding costs.

item.title: Chinese firms' involvement in 5G network may deter investors, EU warns Vietnam
item.description: The involvement of Chinese vendors in the rollout of ‌Vietnam's 5G network may deter foreign companies from investing in the Southeast Asian nation, a top EU official said on Tuesday.

item.title: Spain's Puig shares jump on Estee Lauder merger talks
item.description: Shares of Puig surged ​around 16% on Tuesday, on track for their ‌best trading day on record, after the Spanish beauty group and Estee Lauder said on Monday they were in talks ​over a potential merger.

item.title: Japan's cherry blossom picnics feel the pinch of global inflation
item.description: Global inflation is taking a bite out of Japan's iconic "hanami" cherry blossom picnics, with an index tracking food and ​drink costs up 25% since 2020, private think tank Dai-ichi ‌Life Research Institute said on Tuesday.

item.title: Japan's SMFG plans for possible takeover of Jefferies, FT reports
item.description: Japan's Sumitomo Mitsui Financial Group is working on ​plans for a possible takeover of U.S. investment bank Jefferies , the ‌Financial Times reported on Tuesday.

item.title: Foreign outflows hit Asian stocks as Iran war drives oil shock fears
item.description: Asian stocks have seen heavy foreign outflows so far in March as disruptions to Middle East energy supply from the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran stoked fears of an oil shock ​and stagflation risks.

item.title: SK Hynix may raise up to $10 billion from US listing, Korea Economic Daily says
item.description: South Korea's SK Hynix is considering raising 10 trillion to 15 trillion won ($10.03 billion) through a potential U.S. listing, aiming ​to expand production capacity for advanced memory chips, the ‌Korea Economic Daily reported.

item.title: Feed cost surge from Iran war deepens pain for China's pig farmers
item.description: Rising grain prices fuelled by the Iran war ​are lifting animal feed costs in China, the world's biggest pig market, piling pressure on producers already hit ‌by weak demand and hog prices at 16-year lows.

item.title: Fevertree Drinks' profit drops 16% on Molson Coors' partnership impact, UK levy dispute
item.description: Fevertree Drinks posted a 16% drop in annual ​profit on Tuesday, reflecting the impact ‌of a disputed 2.8-million-pound (3.76 million) packaging levy and margin pressure from the early ​stages of its U.S. partnership ​with Molson Coors .

item.title: Ford to recall over 254,600 U.S. vehicles over issues with assistance features, NHTSA says
item.description: Ford Motor ​is recalling ‌254,640 vehicles in ​the U.S. ​over issues that ⁠may ​result in ​the loss of rearview camera ​image ​and some advanced ‌driver ⁠assistance features, the U.S. National ​Highway ​Traffic ⁠Safety Administration ​said ​on ⁠Tuesday.

item.title: Samsung Electronics, union to resume talks over bonus, union says
item.description: Samsung Electronics' management ​and its South ‌Korean workers' union have agreed to ​resume talks ​on bonuses, the union ⁠said in ​a statement on ​Tuesday.

item.title: Oracle reworks its finance, procurement apps for AI agents
item.description: Oracle is revamping its cloud-based financial software used by large companies to work with artificial intelligence agents, with a goal of having humans ​ask the system business questions and letting AI figure out how to ‌find the data.

item.title: Broadcom flags supply constraints, says TSMC capacity a bottleneck
item.description: Chip designer Broadcom said it is seeing supply chain constraints, including capacity limits at its manufacturing partner TSMC , highlighting the ripple ​effects of soaring demand for AI chips on the broader tech ‌industry.

item.title: Morning Bid: Little relief from Trump
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Rae Wee

item.title: European car sales rise modestly in February, Tesla reverses year-long skid
item.description: New car sales in Europe rose in February after falling in January, while sustained demand for electric vehicles helped Elon ​Musk's all-electric brand Tesla resume growth for the first time since ‌December 2024, official data showed on Tuesday.

item.title: In Iran fallout, US shares hold up better than global rivals, for now
item.description: In the market fallout from the Iran war, U.S. equities have held up better than their counterparts in other global regions, but that relative strength may not shield them from ​more severe declines should the Middle East conflict drag on.

item.title: China positions itself as a stable economic force amid global uncertainty at Beijing forum
item.description: Global corporate executives attending China's flagship annual business conference this week were ​reassured by leaders of the world's second-largest economy that it remains a predictable anchor at a time of geopolitical flux and global ‌uncertainty.

item.title: Exclusive: Amazon says AWS' Bahrain region 'disrupted' following drone activity
item.description: Amazon said on Monday its Amazon Web ​Services region in Bahrain has been "disrupted" amid ‌the current conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: SK Hynix: to buy EUV scanners for $8 billion from ASML Korea
item.description: SK ​Hynix will ‌acquire an extreme ​ultraviolet ​scanner from ASML ⁠Korea ​for 11.95 ​trillion won ($7.97 billion) to ​prepare ​for mass production ‌of ⁠new products, the company ​said ​in ⁠a regulatory ​filing on ​Tuesday.

item.title: Alibaba develops next-gen chip for agentic AI, Chinese media says
item.description: Alibaba on Tuesday revealed its next-generation XuanTie C950 5-nanometer processor at an internal conference on Tuesday, local ​media reported, as the Chinese tech giant gears up ‌for the shift towards agentic AI.
